In 2012, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] competed in the [[Super League XVII|17th season]] of the [[Super League]] and also in the [[2012 Challenge Cup]]. Last season they finished in the top eight before losing in the playoffs.

==Background==

Hull Kingston Rovers enter the 2012 season on the back of a good performance in 2011. They finished in 7th position on the competition table which qualified them for the playoffs before they were eliminated in the first rounds by the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/sep/17/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league|title=Catalan Dragons 56-6 Hull KR | Super League match report|date=17 September 2011|website=The Guardian}}</ref> However, Hull KR have lost much experience from their team over the off season. Key personnel losses included former coach [[Justin Morgan (rugby league)|Justin Morgan]], who had helped Hull KR to get promoted to the Super League and the retirement of captain [[Mick Vella]]. Other key losses include [[Peter Fox (rugby league born 1984)|Peter Fox]], [[Clint Newton]] and [[Shaun Briscoe]].

[[Craig Sandercock]], who had been assistant at the [[Newcastle Knights]]<ref>[http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/default.aspx?s=coach-profile-display&id=1964&player=craig-sandercock&team=Knights]{{dead link|date=September 2016 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> was announced as the new coach in September 2011.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/sep/26/hull-kr-appoint-craig-sandercock|title=Hull KR spring surprise by naming Craig Sandercock as new head coach|date=26 September 2011|website=The Guardian}}</ref> Several players were signed to replace those who had left. These included former [[England national rugby league team|England]] player [[David Hodgson (rugby league)|David Hodgson]], [[Ryan O'Hara]], [[Shannon McDonnell (rugby league)|Shannon McDonnell]] and [[Tongans|Tongan]] international [[Mickey Paea]]. Injuries have also affected Hull KR with the loss of new captain [[Ben Galea]] and their 2011 player of the season [[Blake Green]] for much of the season.

Because of the loss of players, Hull Kingston Rovers prospects were not rated highly for 2012. Dave Woods, writer for the [[BBC]] said that " If they make the top eight, then you'd have to say "top achievement". But I reckon a year of relative struggle could be in store."<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/16786972|title=Super League 2012 guide|work=BBC Sport}}</ref> They were given the 9th best odds to win the competition out of the 14 teams in the Super League.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_6965-hull-kr-2012-season-preview.html |title = Hull KR 2012 season preview  |access-date=4 February 2012 |archive-date=13 May 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140513081855/http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_6965-hull-kr-2012-season-preview.html |url-status=dead }}</ref>

==Season summary==

===February===

Hull KR opened the season by losing 34–16 on the opening day of the season to last season's champions [[Leeds Rhinos]]. The scoreline flattered Leeds who were able to pull away in the last 15 minutes of the match.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/16834123|title=Leeds 34-16 Hull KR|work=BBC Sport}}</ref> The first win of the season came the next week when they defeated Wakefield 22–10 away from home. However, the victory came at a cost with scrum half Michael Dobson suffering a shoulder injury.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/16916505|title=Wakefield 10-22 Hull KR|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
